fields.description:
# RC2b: snapshot-horizon-safe replay (no silent sub-horizon drop)

## Why
When two clones write at once, one can compact (snapshot) and delete the events it folded before it
has seen the other's event. After the histories merge, that other event sorts BEFORE the snapshot,
and replay's positional skip throws it away regardless of `source_event_uuids` -> silent data loss.
The live store still holds ~11 such KNOWN-type orphans.

## The decision (approach) — recommended: Option 1 (+ Option 3 as a cheap first line)
The earlier "apply the orphan / dedup by uuid" idea was refuted: a full-state snapshot overwrites the
orphan, or the stale orphan wrongly wins. Three real options:

- **Option 1 - rebuild the snapshot when a stray shows up (RECOMMENDED).** Keep folded events (A1
  `.retired`) instead of deleting them; when fsck detects a stray pre-snapshot event, rebuild that
  ticket's snapshot from full history so the stray lands in its true position. PROS: actually recovers
  the event in correct order; reuses A1 `.retired` + A3's rebuild path; matches event-sourcing/git-bug
  (snapshot = rebuildable cache). CONS: rebuild costs more than plain replay; needs a trigger.
- **Option 2 - per-field last-write-wins by embedded timestamp (deepest).** Decide field winners by a
  stored per-field write-time, not replay position, so any out-of-order event is safe to apply. PROS:
  fixes the whole class; no rebuild; snapshots can overlay. CONS: biggest change (~5 handlers +
  snapshot schema); most risk. Best as a follow-up, not the first move.
- **Option 3 - don't compact the hot edge (cheapest, weakest).** Only fold events old enough that no
  concurrent writer could still land behind them. PROS: tiny change; standard (Kafka/Cassandra). CONS:
  shrinks but does not close the window -> still probabilistic loss; insufficient alone.

## Scope
Reducer replay + snapshot handling (`reducer/_processors.py` process_snapshot + positional skip
~648-702; `reducer/_sort.py`). NOT the reconciler code. Depends on A1.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Approach confirmed (default: Option 1 + Option 3 first-line) and recorded in an ADR.
- [ ] A merged-in pre-horizon orphan (uuid not in `source_event_uuids`) is no longer silently dropped.
- [ ] Two-clone sub-horizon-append data-loss regression test: RED before, GREEN after.
- [ ] Replay remains a pure function of the on-disk file set (determinism across clones preserved).
